channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S. and Iran launch new strikes after 3 U.S. service members are killed
item.description: A collapsed peace effort gives way to an escalating cycle of U.S. and Iranian retaliation. The U.S. military has confirmed that three service members have been killed since Friday.
item.content:encoded: A collapsed peace effort gives way to an escalating cycle of U.S. and Iranian retaliation. The U.S. military has confirmed that three service members have been killed since Friday.
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: Former national security adviser on the escalating strikes between the U.S. and Iran
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks to former President Biden's national security adviser Jake Sullivan about escalating attacks between the U.S. and Iran and the deaths of U.S. service members.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks to former President Biden's national security adviser Jake Sullivan about escalating attacks between the U.S. and Iran and the deaths of U.S. service members.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: The U.S. carries out a wave of strikes against Iran, as fighting between the two escalates, Congress faces long to-do list as August recess approaches, Andy Burnham becomes U.K.'s 7th prime minister.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. carries out a wave of strikes against Iran, as fighting between the two escalates, Congress faces long to-do list as August recess approaches, Andy Burnham becomes U.K.'s 7th prime minister.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: 'Catch the Devil' shares how a con man traded false information for favors
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel talks to journalist Pamela Colloff about her book "Catch the Devil," a true story of a con man who was a jailhouse informant in nearly 40 criminal cases.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel talks to journalist Pamela Colloff about her book "Catch the Devil," a true story of a con man who was a jailhouse informant in nearly 40 criminal cases.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Spain is the 2026 World Cup champion, defeating Argentina
item.description: Spain was crowned World Cup champion on Sunday, wrapping up a six-week-long tournament co-hosted in Canada, Mexico and the United States.
item.content:encoded: Spain was crowned World Cup champion on Sunday, wrapping up a six-week-long tournament co-hosted in Canada, Mexico and the United States.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: With nursing in high demand, more men look to the field as a stable career option
item.description: Nursing remains an overwhelmingly female occupation. But with nurses in high demand, male college students searching for stable careers are finding their way to the field.
item.content:encoded: Nursing remains an overwhelmingly female occupation. But with nurses in high demand, male college students searching for stable careers are finding their way to the field.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: New study finds avocado oil products may contain cheaper oils
item.description: Sales of avocado oil and products made with it are booming as consumers willingly pay a premium, drawn by the oil's health halo. New research finds many products actually contain other, cheaper oils.
item.content:encoded: Sales of avocado oil and products made with it are booming as consumers willingly pay a premium, drawn by the oil's health halo. New research finds many products actually contain other, cheaper oils.
item.dc:creator: Maria Godoy

item.title: A metro line in Pakistan is transforming the lives of the women who ride it
item.description: A metro line in Pakistan's second-largest city has transformed a city and the lives of the women who ride it.
item.content:encoded: A metro line in Pakistan's second-largest city has transformed a city and the lives of the women who ride it.
item.dc:creator: Diaa Hadid

item.title: With just a week left until August recess, Congress faces long to-do list
item.description: Before the House adjourns for its August recess, House Speaker Mike Johnson wants to advance more defense funding, the president's election integrity bill and a stock trading ban for lawmakers.
item.content:encoded: Before the House adjourns for its August recess, House Speaker Mike Johnson wants to advance more defense funding, the president's election integrity bill and a stock trading ban for lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel

item.title: Republican strategist on the hurdles the GOP faces to pass reconciliation bill
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Republican strategist Rob Burgess about Republican efforts to move forward with a $95 billion party-line budget reconciliation bill.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Republican strategist Rob Burgess about Republican efforts to move forward with a $95 billion party-line budget reconciliation bill.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel
